data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-05-23)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $3.50T, up by 4.49% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $110,408 and $111,980 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $111,149, up by 0.42%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading mixed. Market outperformers include WLD, FET, and KERNEL, up by 19%, 15%, and 13%, respectively.

Top stories of the day:

Solana News: Solana Price Pattern Signals $260 Rally — But $180 Resistance Stands in the Way

XRP News: Volatility Shares Lists First-Ever XRP Futures ETF on Nasdaq Under Ticker ‘XRPI’

Michigan Legislature Proposes New Cryptocurrency Bills

SEC Delays Decision on 21Shares Spot Ethereum ETF Proposal

CFTC Commissioner Confirms Cryptocurrency Perpetual Futures Trading in the U.S.

Michael Saylor Advocates for Bitcoin Retention Amid Dollar Spending

U.S. House to Review Cryptocurrency Market Structure Bill in June

Binance CEO Richard Teng Addresses Concerns Around SUI Ecosystem

data.title: Zhu Su crypto platform OX.FUN reaches cooperation with ORA, with an annualized mining yield of 2000%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Zhu Su, the former co-founder of Three Arrows Capital, announced that his crypto trading platform OX.FUN has reached a strategic cooperation with ORA and officially joined the ORA Web (3,3) smart network ecosystem.
OX/ORA dual-currency Axon has been officially launched. As an LP, you can get ORA mining incentives by providing liquidity. Zhu Su posted on the X platform that due to ORA incentives, the current OX/ORA annualized rate of return (Apy) has reached 2000%.
Driven by the news of the cooperation, ORA rose 13.3% intraday and is now at $0.76. At the same time, OX holders will receive ORA rewards in the future, further binding the ecosystem for common growth.

data.title: A certain address spent $449,000 to buy 826,000 SOON, becoming the top individual SOON holder
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, an address spent $449,000 to buy 826,000 SOON in the past 50 minutes, with an average price of $0.5436, becoming the top individual SOON holder. 
He has currently transferred 700,000 of the tokens to other addresses, and has not taken any other actions.

data.title: SafeMoon Scandal Explodes: CEO Karony Convicted in $9M Crypto Fraud
data.description:
The SafeMoon scandal has escalated dramatically, as the company's CEO, John Karony, has been convicted of orchestrating a cryptocurrency fraud scheme that resulted in losses amounting to an astounding $9 million. This shocking development has sent ripples through the crypto community, raising questions about the integrity of the project and the future of its investors.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/news/safemoon-scam-ex-ceo-faces-45-years

data.title: Gate will launch SOON (SOON) spot and contract trading at 17:00

data.title: DeFi Platforms Offer Cost-Effective Onboarding Compared to Traditional Banks
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms present a significant cost advantage over traditional banks in onboarding new users, as highlighted by Anton Bukov, co-founder of the decentralized exchange 1inch. Speaking at the Dutch Blockchain Week in Amsterdam on May 22, Bukov emphasized that traditional banks incur costs ranging from $100 to $300 per user for document verification and account setup, while online banks spend approximately $20 to $30. In stark contrast, DeFi platforms require minimal resources beyond a smartphone and internet access, making the onboarding process virtually cost-free. Bukov noted, "Onboarding to DeFi literally costs zero. You don’t need brick-and-mortar infrastructure or lengthy verification processes. Just connect and transact." This cost efficiency positions DeFi as a viable solution for reaching the 1.4 billion unbanked individuals worldwide, who remain excluded from traditional financial systems due to high onboarding expenses.

Bukov further elaborated on the potential of DeFi to integrate the unbanked into the global economy, allowing them to engage in real-life transactions using stablecoins like Tether’s USDt (USDT). He pointed out that traditional finance imposes high barriers to entry, whereas DeFi offers a more accessible alternative. As internet access continues to expand globally, DeFi is poised to reach users who have never had access to traditional banking services. Bukov illustrated this by stating, "You can just get a phone, access to the internet, and you can exchange your chicken for USDT," underscoring the ease with which DeFi facilitates participation in the global economy.

In addition to promoting financial inclusion, Bukov highlighted the broader implications of DeFi in providing access to global liquidity. He described crypto as an evolving independent economic zone, where decentralized protocols facilitate the flow of hundreds of billions of dollars. "Crypto isn’t just about adopting stablecoins or building national digital currencies," Bukov remarked. "It’s a growing global liquidity hub." This dynamic liquidity environment fosters financial experimentation, yield strategies, and cross-border capital movement. Bukov also emphasized the economic opportunities available to countries that align their regulations to facilitate access to this global liquidity. "The more countries trade with each other, the more they succeed. Crypto works the same way," he concluded.

data.title: YouTuber's Investigation Exposes Philippine Crypto Scam
data.description: According to Foresight News, a YouTuber's investigative video revealing a crypto scam operation in the Philippines has gone viral, amassing 2.7 million views online. This highlights the ongoing issue of crypto scams in Asia. Additionally, South Korean internet giant Kakao has reported that its security team recently identified over 70,000 fraudulent applications, further underscoring the severity of the crypto scam problem in the region.

data.title: Why is Crypto Ownership Plummeting in Singapore?
data.description:
Crypto ownership among Singaporeans dropped from 40% in 2023 to just 29% in 2024. Despite public awareness reaching an all-time high of 94%, a 11% dip in Singapore residents’ crypto ownership was seen last year. According to a 2025 Independent Reserve Cryptocurrency Index (IRCI) survey, where 1500 residents were surveyed, it was found that nearly.
source: https://99bitcoins.com/news/why-is-crypto-ownership-plummeting-in-singapore/

data.title: Trump Gifts Tron Founder Justin Sun Golden Watch for Being Top $TRUMP Holder
data.description:
Tron founder Justin Sun has received a Trump-branded Golden Tourbillon watch for being the top holder of President Donald Trump’s memecoin.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/trump-gifts-tron-founder-justin-sun-golden-watch-for-being-top-trump-holder/

data.title: Sui launched the whitelist function urgently yesterday to prepare for recovering hacker funds
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Crypto KOL @0x_Todd posted that yesterday Sui urgently launched a whitelist function, and any transaction in the whitelist can skip all security checks (including the number of signatures, permissions, banned objects, etc.). If funds need to be recovered or returned in the future, the official can pre-construct a specific "rescue transaction", so that even if it has been locked by the blacklist, it can bypass all checks and execute once.

data.title: Sui Implements Whitelist Feature for Enhanced Transaction Security
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Sui has introduced a whitelist feature that allows transactions on the list to bypass all security checks, including signature verification, permissions, and blocked entities. This update enables the creation of specific 'rescue transactions' that can be executed even if they are locked by a blacklist, facilitating the recovery or return of funds in the future.

data.title: Why Tether refuses to comply with MiCA
data.description:
Is Tether MiCA compliant? The EU’s new Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation, better known as MiCA, is the first major attempt by a global economic power to create clear, region-wide rules for the crypto space, and stablecoins are a big focus. MiCA mandates best practices. If a stablecoin is going to be traded in the EU, its issuer has to follow some stringent rules:1. You need a licenseTo issue a stablecoin in Europe, you must become a fully authorized electronic money institution (EMI)
source: https://cointelegraph.com/explained/why-tether-refuses-to-comply-with-mica?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
